description_long: This is a small, single-propeller airplane with a yellow fuselage accented by red wingtips and tail surfaces. The aircraft features a bubble canopy cockpit, likely seating one or two occupants, and is marked with military-style insignia and the text 'KON. MARINE' along the side. The wings are straight and low-mounted, with visible ailerons and a prominent registration number '197' on the upper wing surface. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear appears fixed and is configured in a tricycle arrangement, with two main wheels and a nose wheel. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the engine cowling is painted black, contrasting with the rest of the fuselage.
description: A small, yellow single-propeller airplane with red accents, military markings, and a bubble canopy cockpit. It features low-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and fixed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the yellow fuselage, red tail and wingtips, 'KON. MARINE' text, and the single propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight low-mounted wings, red wingtips, and the bubble canopy cockpit.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the engine cowling, propeller, and the registration number '197' on the wing.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the propeller, nose wheel, and the symmetrical wing layout with red tips.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed bubble canopy cockpit, likely single or tandem seat with clear side and top windows.
